mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions, lowers discount rates, and supports equity multiples, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when policy is clearly easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than baseline during periods when FEDFUNDS is trending down for at least 2 consecutive monthly observations, or if triggered samples show negative median returns.

mechanism_narrative: A high and rising FEDFUNDS rate increases real discount rates and tightens liquidity, which pressures equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation remains above 3%
failure_condition: Reject if SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in a majority of high-rate/high-inflation trigger episodes, or if the median triggered return is not below the unconditional median by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals weakening household income and demand, so consumer-sensitive equities should underperform over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not negative on average after the unemployment trigger, or if rising UNRATE episodes do not coincide with below-baseline equity returns in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while UNRATE stays low, it signals broad real activity without labor stress, supporting earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of higher INDPRO and low UNRATE does not produce positive 60-day forward returns, or if either series alone explains the signal and the interaction term adds no incremental predictive value.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumer and business spending, which lifts revenue expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth turns positive after at least 2 months of deceleration
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS growth episodes do not lead to higher-than-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the effect is unstable across successive walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST reflects weakening housing demand and financing sensitivity, which tends to precede broader cyclical risk-off behavior and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average while FEDFUNDS is above 5%
failure_condition: Reject if the combined low-housing/high-rate condition does not generate negative 20-day returns more often than not, or if the signal disappears once FEDFUNDS is controlled for.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE marks labor-market deterioration that undermines earnings outlooks and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-UNRATE combination fails to predict negative 60-day returns, or if negative returns are no more frequent than in non-triggered samples.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery, rising GDPC1 implies stronger aggregate demand and improving corporate revenue expectations, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if quarters with rising GDPC1 do not precede above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the relationship reverses in rolling out-of-sample tests.
